---
title: "CodeBook Lab"
date: "2026"
date-format: "YYYY"
abstract: "A Python package for computational social science that benchmarks local LLM annotation runs against human labels across model choice, model size, prompt style, zero-shot versus few-shot learning, and sampling hyperparameters."
hide-abstract: true
packagelink: "https://pypi.org/project/codebook-lab/"
codelink: "https://github.com/LorcanMcLaren/codebook-lab"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: article
    template-partials:
      - "../partials/title-block.html"
---

CodeBook Lab is a Python package for running LLM annotation experiments against human-coded benchmark data. It takes a codebook and labelled dataset exported from [CodeBook Studio](codebook-studio.qmd) and lets researchers evaluate local models systematically across the dimensions that matter for text-as-data research:

- **Model choice and model size**: compare any models available through Ollama
- **Prompt style**: standard, persona, or chain-of-thought wrappers
- **Zero-shot versus few-shot learning**: toggle codebook examples on or off
- **Sampling hyperparameters**: sweep over temperature and top-p settings

Experiments are controlled from Python, so users can start with a single run, scale up to multiple experiment specifications, or iterate through larger parameter grids when needed. Because the codebook and labelled data stay constant across runs, each dimension can be isolated and compared against the same human labels.

```{=html}
<style>
.codebook-flow {
  --codebook-border: #7a5a65;
  --codebook-card-bg: #f7f1ec;
  --codebook-data-bg: #fcf8f5;
  --codebook-title: #5f3b4a;
  --codebook-text: #4a3a40;
  --codebook-code-bg: rgba(95, 59, 74, 0.08);
  --codebook-code-border: rgba(122, 90, 101, 0.22);
  --codebook-shadow: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: minmax(220px, 1fr) 80px minmax(220px, 1fr);
  gap: 1.25rem;
  align-items: center;
  margin: 1.5rem 0 1rem 0;
}

.quarto-dark .codebook-flow {
  --codebook-border: #7b9bb5;
  --codebook-card-bg: #253544;
  --codebook-data-bg: #1f2d3a;
  --codebook-title: #b7d0e4;
  --codebook-text: #d7e6f3;
  --codebook-code-bg: #13212d;
  --codebook-code-border: rgba(183, 208, 228, 0.18);
  --codebook-shadow: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.22);
}

.codebook-card {
  border: 2px solid var(--codebook-border);
  border-radius: 12px;
  background: var(--codebook-card-bg);
  color: var(--codebook-text);
  padding: 1rem 1.1rem;
  box-shadow: 0 2px 8px var(--codebook-shadow);
}

.codebook-card h3 {
  margin: 0 0 0.6rem 0;
  font-size: 1.15rem;
  color: var(--codebook-title);
}

.codebook-card ul {
  margin: 0;
  padding-left: 1.1rem;
}

.codebook-card li {
  margin-bottom: 0.35rem;
  color: var(--codebook-text);
}

.codebook-arrow {
  display: flex;
  justify-content: center;
  align-items: center;
  color: var(--codebook-title);
  font-size: 2rem;
  font-weight: 700;
}

.codebook-bridge {
  grid-column: 1 / -1;
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1fr 80px 1fr;
  gap: 1.25rem;
  align-items: center;
  margin-top: -0.25rem;
}

.codebook-data {
  border: 1.5px dashed var(--codebook-border);
  border-radius: 10px;
  background: var(--codebook-data-bg);
  padding: 0.85rem 1rem;
  color: var(--codebook-text);
}

.codebook-data strong {
  color: var(--codebook-title);
}

.codebook-card code,
.codebook-card li code,
.codebook-data code {
  color: var(--codebook-text);
  background: var(--codebook-code-bg);
  border: 1px solid var(--codebook-code-border);
  border-radius: 0.45rem;
  padding: 0.08rem 0.35rem;
}

.quarto-dark .codebook-card code,
.quarto-dark .codebook-card li code,
.quarto-dark .codebook-data code {
  color: var(--codebook-text) !important;
  background: var(--codebook-code-bg) !important;
  border-color: var(--codebook-code-border) !important;
}

@media (max-width: 900px) {
  .codebook-flow,
  .codebook-bridge {
    grid-template-columns: 1fr;
  }

  .codebook-arrow {
    transform: rotate(90deg);
  }
}
</style>

<div class="codebook-flow">
  <div class="codebook-card">
    <h3>CodeBook Studio</h3>
    <ul>
      <li>Define the annotation task</li>
      <li>Annotate texts with humans</li>
      <li>Export <code>codebook.json</code> and <code>ground-truth.csv</code></li>
    </ul>
  </div>

  <div class="codebook-arrow" aria-hidden="true">→</div>

  <div class="codebook-card">
    <h3>CodeBook Lab</h3>
    <ul>
      <li>Run LLM annotation experiments</li>
      <li>Isolate model, prompt, and hyperparameter effects</li>
      <li>Score outputs against human labels</li>
    </ul>
  </div>

  <div class="codebook-bridge">
    <div class="codebook-data">
      <strong>Shared inputs</strong><br>
      <code>codebook.json</code> + human-annotated <code>ground-truth.csv</code>
    </div>

    <div class="codebook-arrow" aria-hidden="true">→</div>

    <div class="codebook-data">
      <strong>Experiment outputs</strong><br>
      Accuracy, agreement, runtime, energy, and emissions metrics
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

CodeBook Lab also tracks runtime, prompt and response length, energy consumption, and estimated carbon emissions for each run, with aggregate metrics logged to a single CSV for cross-experiment comparison.

The package ships with a bundled starter task so you can test the workflow before plugging in your own data. For a step-by-step walkthrough covering both tools, see the [CodeBook Studio & Lab Tutorial](../codebook-tutorial.qmd).
